title: AGD allometric baseline
claim: AGD scales with body size across species, sex, and developmental stage.
formal_statement: AGD ~ body_mass + body_length + sex + age_stage + species
expected_direction: positive allometric scaling with body size
primary_test: log-log allometric regression with sex and age-stage stratification
replication_threshold: consistent allometric direction across at least two independent species groups

title: AGD residuals covary with craniofacial residuals
claim: AGD residuals covary with snout or skull proportion after correcting for body size, sex, species, age, measurement quality, and phylogeny.
formal_statement: corr(resid(AGD ~ body_size + sex + age + species + phylogeny), resid(face_ratio ~ body_size + sex + age + species + phylogeny)) != 0
expected_direction: to be estimated; no directional assumption in v0.1
primary_test: partial correlation or regression of residualized AGD and craniofacial ratios
replication_threshold: independent support in at least two datasets or species groups

title: Neonatal geometry and later fertility
claim: Neonatal developmental geometry contributes predictive information about later reproductive outcomes when combined with birthweight, head circumference, gestational age, and puberty timing.
formal_statement: fertility_outcome ~ neonatal_AGD + birthweight + head_circumference + gestational_age + puberty_timing + confounders
expected_direction: developmental geometry improves prediction beyond single neonatal measures
replication_threshold: longitudinal support in an independent cohort or animal model

title: Domestication developmental deformation
claim: Domesticated or selectively bred lineages show coupled shifts in craniofacial geometry, reproductive timing, stress physiology, and reproductive morphology.
formal_statement: developmental_coordinates ~ generation + selection_pressure + breed + body_size + sex + age
expected_direction: selective breeding deforms multiple developmental coordinates together
primary_test: generation-aware mixed model of reproductive and craniofacial coordinates
replication_threshold: consistent coupled shifts in at least two domesticated or selectively bred systems

title: Fertility treatment response manifold
claim: Infertility treatment outcomes are better predicted by developmental-state profiles than by conventional fertility markers alone.
expected_direction: developmental-state coordinates improve predictive performance over conventional baseline models
primary_test: baseline versus enhanced model comparison using aggregate or controlled-access data
replication_threshold: independent aggregate or controlled-access clinic replication
